one_line_ask: Turn the client's raw product shot, lifestyle backdrop, and logo into a performance-ready Meta ad creative kit: a retouched pure-white-bg hero, a brand-graded variant, three platform-ratio outpainted canvases (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16), a vectorized logo, and a Firefly review board.
brief:
DONE is a DTC homeware brand launching paid-social tests for its matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper and needs a freelancer to build the imagery layer of a Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad-creative kit, faithful to the brand guidelines (brand teal #0F6E64 on warm off-white #F4EFE7). You are supplied four client assets: (1) a raw studio product photo of the dripper on a near-white sweep that still shows seamless-paper shadows and a faint gray cast; (2) a lifestyle/kitchen backdrop plate for context variants; (3) the DONE wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (4) the brand-guideline sheet as a PDF for reference. 

Build the production chain entirely on Adobe connector tools: ingest the assets into a working folder; auto-correct the studio shot's geometry and tone; isolate the product and force a true, pure-white e-commerce background (catalog-clean, no gray cast, no stray shadow halo); push a brand-consistent color grade so the ceramic reads warm and premium and the teal accents stay on-palette; then generate the platform canvases performance marketers actually test — a 1:1 feed square, a 4:5 portrait feed, and a 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical — using AI outpaint to extend the clean backgro
